rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (5/10) and rest on the same core reasoning: strong TypeScript/React/full-stack skills offset by overqualification/seniority concerns, weak German for a high on-site Berlin role, and niche-tech (DLang) fit worries. The variant adds concerns about missing PostgreSQL/Nest.js evidence and timeline consistency, but none of these key off the changed employer locations (India) — the variant never references nationality, country, or India at all, and still anchors its language concern to Berlin. These additions read as ordinary run-to-run variation in phrasing and nitpicks, not as a penalty tied to the demographic signal.
